profile.name: Evan Pang
profile.summary: As an intern in the IT Department at Tertiary Infotech Academy PTE LTD, I maintained and improved the company's training website and administration platform. My work focused on making course-management interfaces clearer, presenting course content correctly, validating platform functions and documenting issues for follow-up. These experiences are developing my skills in front-end implementation, application support, testing and practical problem solving.

company.name: Tertiary Infotech Academy PTE LTD
company.description: Tertiary Courses Academy Pte Ltd is a training institution established in Singapore, offering a wide range of modular, skill-based courses across various industries, including Infocomm Technology, Digital Media, Electronics, Horticulture, and Business Administration.
company.department_description: The IT department is responsible for overseeing technology-related tasks and ensuring the proper functioning of an organization's technology infrastructure

job_scope: Do the tasks assigned by the supervisor and complete them on time.
Maintain the website for the company and update the content as needed.
Fix visual and functional issues on the website to ensure a smooth user experience.
Collaborate with team members and communicate effectively to achieve project goals.
portfolio_thesis: Ensuring the quality and functionality of the company's website through effective maintenance, updates, and issue resolution, while contributing to the overall user experience and supporting the department's objectives.

projects.title: Course Management User Interface Improvements
projects.summary: The course administration screens contained inconsistent control placement, exposed HTML in course content and presentation issues that made information harder to manage and read. The objective was to improve the usability and visual consistency of the course editor and the public course page.
projects.responsibility: I reviewed the interface issues highlighted in the task screenshots, made the assigned visual and content-presentation changes, and checked the affected admin and public-facing screens. My contribution covered course sessions, assessment methods, lesson topics, training modes, documents, course-topic formatting and page spacing.
projects.approach: I compared the marked problem areas with the revised screens, worked through each course-management section systematically and checked that controls remained clear and usable. I also verified the content in both editing and display contexts, including the editor/HTML views and the public learning-outcomes page.
projects.outcome: An 11-screen evidence set records a clearer and more consistent course-management experience. The revised views show structured topic controls, better-positioned actions, formatted course content instead of exposed HTML markup, and improved spacing and readability on the public course page.

projects.title: Training Platform Functionality Enhancements and Validation
projects.summary: The training platform required functional checks and targeted updates across its public website and administration portal. The work covered course registration and email confirmation, reviews, store views, profile settings, funding options, categories, schedules, assessment links, dashboard tools and course-image handling.
projects.responsibility: I supported the assigned functionality changes by reproducing issues, checking the relevant admin configuration or user workflow, and validating the resulting screen or notification. I also documented unresolved defects, including a missing course image, so that the problem and its location were clear.
projects.approach: I tested each function in its real context, moving between the public storefront, administration screens and generated email output. I used the annotated screenshots to identify the target area, checked configuration-dependent changes across store views, refreshed cached content where required and recorded visible evidence of the result.
projects.outcome: A 12-screen evidence set provides a traceable record of the platform checks and enhancements. It verifies working or configurable functions such as registration confirmation, review administration, profile and theme controls, funding eligibility, category management, editable course schedules and assessment links, while separately recording the course-image defect for correction.

artifacts.title: Course Interface Change Screenshot Record
artifacts.summary: This evidence supports Project 01 by showing the course editor and public course page before and after the assigned interface and content-presentation changes.
artifacts.deliverable: An 11-screen visual record covering course-session actions, assessment-method presentation, lesson-topic controls, virtual-meeting and document sections, training modes, footer presentation, course-topic editing and the public learning-outcomes layout.
artifacts.contribution: I reviewed the marked interface problems, implemented or checked the assigned corrections and organised the visible results into a project evidence set.
artifacts.use: A supervisor or assessor can use the screenshots to connect each highlighted interface issue with the revised screen and evaluate the clarity of the final presentation.
artifacts.action: I inspected each highlighted area, corrected layout and content-display behaviour, checked related controls and compared the administration view with the public course page. I paid particular attention to the difference between raw HTML input and correctly rendered course content.
artifacts.result: The evidence confirms that course topics are presented as formatted content rather than visible HTML markup, topic and session controls are organised more clearly, and the public page has more readable content spacing.
artifacts.corrective_action: The raw-HTML display and inconsistent presentation were corrected in the revised views. A future iteration should capture every item as a consistent before-and-after pair and repeat the checks at mobile and tablet widths.

skills.name: User Interface Implementation
skills.evidence: I applied and validated interface changes across multiple related course-management sections while maintaining a consistent visual structure and usable controls.

skills.name: Problem Solving
skills.evidence: I used the annotated problem areas to isolate presentation issues, compare the editing and display contexts and verify that the revised result addressed the visible problem.

screenshots.caption: Course Sessions and Additional Documents controls reviewed for clearer placement.
Assessment-method options organised as clear selectable cards.
Structured lesson-topic controls for adding, ordering, expanding and removing topics.
Problem state: course content displayed as raw HTML inside the editor.
Revised state: formatted course content with Editor, HTML and Clean Up controls.
Public course page checked for readable paragraph and learning-outcome spacing.

artifacts.title: Platform Functionality Test and Change Log
artifacts.summary: This evidence supports Project 02 by recording functional checks, configuration work and visible outcomes across the training platform's public and administrative workflows.
artifacts.deliverable: A 12-screen evidence log covering dashboard tools, course-image display, registration confirmation email, review editing, store-view visibility, cache management, profile and theme controls, email templates, funding eligibility, course categories, schedule editing and assessment links.
artifacts.contribution: I reproduced the assigned issues, worked through the relevant platform screens, checked the resulting behaviour and documented both validated functions and items that still required correction.
artifacts.use: The evidence gives the IT team and assessor a concise visual record of what was checked, where the function is managed and whether the screenshot shows a working result or an identified defect.
artifacts.action: I followed each workflow from its entry point, reviewed the related settings or data, checked multi-store and cache-dependent behaviour where relevant, and validated visible outputs such as the registration email and editable course schedule.
artifacts.result: The evidence confirms access to and operation of several key functions, including registration confirmation, review administration, profile and theme settings, funding choices, category management, schedule editing and assessment-link configuration. It also clearly identifies the missing course image as a separate defect requiring follow-up.
artifacts.corrective_action: I distinguished confirmed outcomes from unresolved issues and refreshed cached content when configuration changes required it. For future testing, I would record an expected result, actual result and pass/fail status for each screen and redact all personal or internal data before publication.

skills.name: Application Support and Testing
skills.evidence: I tested connected functions across the storefront, administration portal and email output, and separated verified behaviour from defects that required further action.

skills.name: Problem Solving
skills.evidence: I traced each annotated issue to the relevant workflow or configuration screen, checked dependencies such as store view and cache state, and documented a clear result for follow-up.

screenshots.caption: Data Analytics Tools dashboard checked for access to the required tool areas.
Identified defect: the Python course card did not load its course image.
Cache-management screen used when validating configuration-dependent changes.
Funding-eligibility choices and discount labels checked on the registration form.
Course-category administration and category structure reviewed.
Editable course dates, prices and sort order verified in Course Schedule.

knowledge.name: Web Interface and Content Rendering
knowledge.explanation: I understand that web content must work in both its editing context and its final display context. This includes the relationship between HTML markup, formatted content, layout hierarchy, control placement, readability and responsive presentation across administration and public-facing pages.
knowledge.demonstration: In Project 01, I compared a course-topics view that exposed raw HTML with the revised formatted editor and public course page. This helped me verify that the content was not only stored correctly but also presented in a readable and usable form.

knowledge.name: Training Platform Administration Workflow
knowledge.explanation: I learned how different parts of a training platform support the course-delivery process, including course categories, schedules, funding choices, store views, reviews, registration notifications, assessment links and cache-dependent updates.
knowledge.demonstration: In Project 02, I followed functions across the public storefront, administration portal and registration email output. For example, I checked editable course schedules in the admin area and verified the visible registration confirmation produced by the platform.

skills.name: User Interface Implementation
skills.explanation: In my role, user interface implementation means applying and checking visual changes that make website content and controls clearer, more consistent and easier to use without disrupting the surrounding workflow.
skills.demonstration: I worked through the highlighted course-management screens, improved or validated the placement and presentation of session, topic, assessment, training-mode and document controls, and checked the resulting course content on the public page.

skills.name: Application Support and Functional Testing
skills.explanation: Application support and functional testing involve reproducing an issue, locating the related workflow or configuration, checking dependencies and recording whether the actual result matches the intended behaviour.
skills.demonstration: I checked functions across dashboard tools, course registration, reviews, store views, profile settings, funding options, categories, schedules and assessment links. I recorded confirmed results separately from the unresolved missing course-image defect so that follow-up action remained clear.

skills.name: Problem Solving and Communication
skills.explanation: Website issues are often reported visually or as a short task description, so I needed to interpret the problem, investigate the relevant screen and communicate the result in a way that another person could verify.
skills.demonstration: I used the annotated arrows and screenshots to isolate each target area, checked the related admin or public workflow and produced a traceable evidence record. I also described the broken course image as an unresolved defect instead of presenting it as a completed fix.

attitudes.name: Learning Agility and Ownership
attitudes.explanation: Learning agility and ownership mean becoming familiar with new platform areas quickly, following an assigned issue through to validation and taking responsibility for documenting anything that still needs attention.
attitudes.demonstration: I moved between unfamiliar course-management, store-view, cache, review, profile, category and scheduling screens and used each screen's context to complete or validate the assigned task. When the evidence showed a remaining image defect, I retained it as a follow-up item.

attitudes.name: Attention to Detail and Professionalism
attitudes.explanation: Attention to detail is important when small differences in content formatting, store scope, cache state or control placement can affect the user experience. Professionalism also requires protecting personal and internal information in portfolio evidence.
attitudes.demonstration: I checked both problem and result states carefully, distinguished verified functions from unresolved issues and selected portfolio screenshots that exclude personal names, registration details and direct assessment-document links.

reflection.nature_of_work: I maintained the company website, fixed visual and functional issues, and checked that my changes worked correctly.
reflection.challenge: The main challenge was that one change could affect the admin portal, public website, forms or emails. Some changes also depended on the selected store view or website cache.
reflection.action: I used the annotated screenshots to find each issue, checked the related settings or page, tested the result and recorded any problem that still needed work.
reflection.result: I improved the course-management interface and checked several platform functions. My 23 screenshots show the completed work as well as the missing course image that still required follow-up.
reflection.critical_evaluation: The screenshots show my work, but some do not include a clear before-and-after comparison. This makes a few changes harder to verify.
reflection.alternative: Next time, I would use a simple test log with the issue, expected result, actual result, status and before-and-after screenshots.
reflection.learning_growth: I improved my understanding of website interfaces, content formatting, admin settings and functional testing. I also became more careful when checking results and documenting issues.
reflection.accomplishment: I am proud that I completed a range of website improvements and organised the work into two clear project records.
reflection.future_self_title: A dependable web and application support professional
reflection.future_self: I want to develop my skills in front-end development, application support and software testing.
next_steps: Maybe Return to School to do more courses in front-end development or software testing.
Maybe look for a full-time role in front-end development or software quality assurance.

contact_note: I welcome opportunities to discuss my internship projects, web and application support experience, and future roles in front-end development or software quality assurance.
privacy_note: This portfolio contains only approved or redacted workplace information. Sensitive, proprietary and personal data have been excluded.
